mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier discount rates and lower hurdle rates for equities, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month when the policy rate is being cut rather than held steady.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03 during easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in PAYEMS reflects stronger payroll growth and household income momentum, which supports consumption and raises near-term equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by at least 100k over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if payroll-expansion triggers are followed by negative or zero average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal fails in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and better revenue growth for consumer-facing firms, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not coincide with above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign of the relationship flips in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contemporaneous decline in INDPRO combined with elevated CPIAUCSL implies supply-side strain and stagflation pressure, which compresses margins and weighs on equities over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-down and CPI-high condition does not produce negative 20-day returns more often than not, or if either series alone explains the signal as well as the pair.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor demand and impending earnings deterioration, so a higher unemployment rate should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are not followed by negative average 20-day returns, or if triggered periods deliver positive returns in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous upswing in GDPC1 and HOUST signals broad final demand and housing-led cyclicality, which should support equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP-and-housing strength does not improve 60-day forward returns relative to baseline, or if the pair adds no predictive power beyond GDP alone.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already high and CPIAUCSL remains elevated, the market should anticipate further tightening and lower valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1.0% and CPIAUCSL inflation trend stays positive for 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate, high-inflation triggers do not precede negative 20-day returns, or if the observed hit rate is not materially below 50% for positive-return outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: A softening in RSAFS together with weaker PAYEMS indicates fading consumer income and spending momentum, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next week before earnings revisions fully adjust.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls and PAYEMS growth decelerates for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if combined retail-sales and payroll weakness does not predict negative 5-day returns, or if the relationship disappears after controlling for either series individually.
